channel.title: Terms on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Terms on emsenn.net

item.title: Anarchist grammar
item.description: Anarchist grammar is the rhetorical sequence that anarchist political speech follows regardless of its surface content — a six-step structure that mirror-inverts the corresponding steps of fascist grammar. Where fascist grammar declares crisis, names a pure community, denounces enemies, reframes suffering as sacrifice, promises destiny, and asserts inevitability, anarchist grammar refuses each move with a structurally specific countermove. The framework’s analysis is that the inversion does not constitute an exit from the operational closure within which fascist grammar operates; anarchist grammar inhabits the same metabolism as another available genre.

item.title: Attributing
item.description: To attribute is to assign a property, identity, or condition to a being or situation. The act looks descriptive — she is depressed; they are racist; this is trauma; that is collapse — and the framework’s claim is that under ontogenring the act is not descriptive but constitutive. Attributing installs the named being or situation into a genre-formatted ontology, with the ontogenric entitlements and anticipations that follow from the installation arriving with it.

item.title: Californian projection
item.description: Californian projection is the formation in which California — the actual coastal territory and the imaginative screen of the same name — has served as the historical laboratory in which the apparatus iterates. The Misiones Californianas, the Gold Rush, Hollywood, Esalen, Silicon Valley, the wellness industry: each is a stage of one continuous apparatus, not a separate phenomenon. The land is not California; California is what we make of it. The projection is the apparatus.

item.title: Collapse as narrativization
item.description:
Collapse as narrativization is the result of ontogenring acting on destabilization. The structural condition produced by saturation does not arrive at subjects as a structural condition; it arrives genre-formatted, with a name (collapse), an affective register (dread, mourning, resolve), a temporal shape (decline, fall, possibility-of-renewal), and a set of subject-positions ready to occupy each phase (the prepper, the doomer, the chronicler, the mourner, the rebuilder).

¶Etymology and discursive history

The word collapse derives from Latin collabi — col- (together) + labi (to slip, fall) — to fall together, to fall in one body. The medieval and early-modern uses are physical and mechanical: a structure collapses; a cave collapses; a body collapses. The civilizational sense — collapse as the structural breakdown of a society — emerges in the eighteenth century with Gibbon’s account of Rome, but does not become a popular discursive formation until the twentieth.

item.title: Crisis-settlerism
item.description: Crisis-settlerism is the intensification of settlerism under conditions of structural saturation and destabilization. When the apparatus’s contradictions become visible, settler subject-positions are reproduced more aggressively, more aestheticized, and more violent. The legitimating erasures that operate quietly in ordinary settlerism — terra nullius, the Doctrine of Discovery, the frontier myth — shift toward overt assertion of the right to displace. The formation does not invent itself in crisis; it is settlerism’s pre-existing structure operating under conditions where its work is no longer concealable.

item.title: Destabilization
item.description:
Destabilization is what saturation induces. The system loses the prior equilibrium that absorbed its constitutive contradictions; the routings that previously bridged the gap between promise and production stop converging; what had been routine — the daily reproductions of subjects, infrastructures, expectations — begins to register as crisis. Destabilization is not a single event; it is the new ambient condition in which events are encountered.

¶Lineage and adjacent traditions

The underlying dynamics have been studied across disciplines, and destabilization in the framework’s sense draws from each.

item.title: Expert naïveté
item.description: Expert naïveté is the formation in which a person publicly recognized as expert in one domain, induced into novel learning by crisis, performs expertise on adjacent topics they have only just begun to learn — with the audience reading the new material from the prior expert position rather than from the actual depth of understanding behind it. The mismatch is the formation’s signature: the audience attributes a depth that the producer does not in fact have, and the producer’s own framing typically does not correct the misattribution.

item.title: Liberal ontologizing
item.description:
Liberal ontologizing is the foundational operation of liberal thought. It treats the things of the world — persons, peoples, properties, values — as substances with inherent attributes, existing prior to and independently of the relations they participate in. The world that liberal ontologizing produces is a world of pre-political individuals, pre-relational entities, sovereign units that then enter into the social, the political, and the economic.

The operation is not a description of how things actually are; it is a metaphysical posture that has the appearance of being a neutral default. To denaturalize it is the first move of any analysis that does not take its products for granted.

item.title: Liberalism legitimacy crisis
item.description: The Liberalism Legitimacy Crisis is the named structural moment in which liberalism’s constitutive contradictions become experientially insoluble for its subjects rather than locally manageable, and the system shifts from promising resolution of those contradictions to promising regulation of the symptoms. The system loses legitimacy as an ideological system in the specific sense that it can no longer produce the resolutions it has historically promised; what it can still produce is administrative management.

item.title: Ontogenring
item.description: Ontogenring is the process by which subjects are produced through genre-formatting. Where genring reduces a living people to a fixed literary type — selecting, codifying, and substituting features until the type replaces the people — ontogenring extends the same operation up one level, to the production of subjects themselves. Beings are no longer merely placed in genres; their being is constituted by the genre. The genre is the ontology. Hence the onto- prefix: it is not only that experiences are generic, it is that what one is is generic.

item.title: Platformal liberalism
item.description: Platformal liberalism is the operating mode of liberalism in which platforms — digital platforms primarily, but also platform-style logics that have spread beyond software — become the primary medium of subject-formation, contradiction-management, and the distribution of feeling rules. Liberalism’s central operations are no longer carried out by states, parties, churches, or schools as the dominant institutions; they are carried out by platforms, with the older institutions reorganizing themselves around platform logics in order to remain operative.

item.title: Saturation
item.description: Saturation is the structural condition in which a system can no longer absorb additional load without strain. The variety of disturbances arriving from the system’s environment has begun to exceed the variety of internal states the system can use to absorb them. Under unsaturated conditions, the system extends — adapts internal variety to match external variety, expands its repertoire, defers what it cannot immediately resolve. Under saturation, the extending stops working; the system is receiving more than it can metabolize.

item.title: Settlerism
item.description: Settlerism is the structural formation of governance organized around continuous displacement-and-replacement of indigenous relations, the production of stable settler subject-positions on a continuously-renewed colonial frontier, and the legal-cultural infrastructures that legitimate the displacement by erasing indigenous prior presence. It is a structure rather than an event, a regime rather than an episode — settler arrival is not the founding moment of the formation but its first iteration, with each subsequent generation continuing the displacement under different vocabularies.

item.title: Genre coherence
item.description: Genre coherence is the structural demand that a nation’s cast of folk types remain stable. Once a nation imagines itself as a collection of authentic genre figures — the Noble Indian, the Hearty Bavarian, the Mountain Kurd, the salt-of-the-earth farmer — the integrity of the national story depends on the characters remaining as assigned. Anyone who does not fit the genre becomes a problem for the story. Not necessarily an ideological enemy, but a narrative disruption: a character who breaks the fourth wall.

item.title: Genring
item.description: Genring is the reduction of a living people to a fixed literary type. It is not a metaphor for stereotyping but a specific political operation with three steps: selection (extracting visually striking, emotionally resonant features from a people’s life — dress, diet, habitat, ritual), codification (assembling those features into a stable character template and circulating it through novels, performances, images, and festivals until it becomes self-reinforcing), and substitution (the template replaces the people, so that encountering the people means encountering the character).

item.title: Temporal middle
item.description:
The temporal middle is the position of inheriting, continuing, and transforming a tradition without either renewing it wholesale or abandoning it. Every living person occupies this position. No one is purely “traditional” (frozen in the past) or purely “modern” (severed from it). People inherit what they inherit, transform what they can, and continue. The framework names the position because the structures it analyzes systematically eliminate it.

¶Etymology and conceptual sources

The expression temporal middle foregrounds the temporal dimension that purity-binaries collapse. Tradition and modernity, as ordinarily deployed, are time-coded: tradition is the past inhabited in the present; modernity is the present that has separated from the past. The middle position — present that is continuing the past — is invisible within the binary, because the binary requires that one moment in time be cleanly distinguished from another.

item.title: Genre calibration
item.description:
Genre calibration is the continuous, distributed tuning by which expectations are adjusted so that structural crisis is experienced as personal narrative. The concept synthesizes three theoretical bodies into a single operation: Berlant’s account of genre as structure of anticipation, Ahmed’s account of how affects circulate and orient bodies, and Wiener’s cybernetic account of feedback that maintains stability without central coordination.

A person loses their job during a recession. In the self-help genre, this is a “pivot” — an opportunity to reinvent yourself. In the activist genre, it is evidence of systemic exploitation. In the therapeutic genre, it is a wound requiring healing. The recession is the same recession. The job loss is the same job loss. What changes is the genre — the set of expectations determining what the event means, what feelings are appropriate, and what counts as resolution. Genre calibration names the active process by which these expectations are tuned for which subject under which conditions.

item.title: Californication
item.description: Californication is the operation by which liberal governance reproduces itself by formatting structural contradictions as personally manageable affect. The system does not persuade its subjects of its legitimacy and does not, principally, coerce them; it formats them. Crises that liberalism cannot resolve are routed through subjects as mood, trauma, growth opportunity, or lifestyle, and the self-regulation that follows is itself the governance mechanism. The subject who meditates through a housing crisis, journals through a pandemic, or microdoses through ecological collapse is not failing to respond politically — they are doing exactly what the system requires: converting structural incoherence into self-regulatory behavior.

item.title: Fascist grammar
item.description: Fascist grammar is the rhetorical sequence that fascist political speech follows regardless of its surface content. The concept distinguishes grammar from ideology: an ideology is a set of beliefs, but a grammar is a structure that generates statements regardless of their specific content. Fascist grammar can generate fascist politics, but it can also generate postliberal Catholic integralism, left-wing populism, or liberal crisis rhetoric. The grammar is the deep structure. The politics is the surface.

item.title: Harm governance
item.description: Harm governance is the institutional-affective regime in which care has been collapsed into risk management. Political action must be administered through proper procedural channels in order to count as legitimate, and people who have lost faith in the state reproduce its administrative logic with each other. The signature feature is an asymmetry: individual direct action registers as inherently risky because it lacks institutional validation, while mass action registers as safe because institutional form makes it feel legitimate. The framework cannot recognize that institutional form itself introduces risks — of co-optation, of slowness, of dependence on the structures being challenged.

item.title: Industrial intellectualism
item.description: Industrial intellectualism is the structural condition in which intellectual labor is organized as a production process. Thought is converted into narrative products — threads, essays, papers, podcasts, newsletters — packaged for recognizability within a genre and evaluated by whether the analysis feels right, not by whether it corresponds to material outcomes. The term names not a type of person or a moral failing but a condition: the condition in which intellectual work, regardless of the worker’s intentions, is formatted by its market of circulation.

item.title: Neurotic platformal intellectual
item.description:
The neurotic platformal intellectual is the structural subject-position produced when platforms industrialize the confessional incitement-to-discourse. Every user becomes a performer of expertise whose intellectual labor is governed by anxiety, feeling rules, and platform-native logics of visibility. The position is not a type of person but a structural condition that anyone participating in platform discourse occupies to some degree.

Someone posts a detailed analysis of monetary policy on a social media platform. Within hours, people with no economics training are debating the analysis in replies — not asking questions but offering corrections, alternative theories, and confident disagreements. No one does this with surgery. When a surgeon posts about a procedure, non-surgeons do not show up to debate incision technique. But policy analysis on a platform is discourse, and discourse is everyone’s medium. The platform makes no structural distinction between someone who has spent years studying monetary systems and someone who read a thread about inflation that morning. What it registers is engagement.

item.title: Savior-slave subject
item.description: The savior-slave subject is the dual subject-position californication produces: the subject is constituted as personally responsible for managing structural crises (savior) and structurally incapable of transforming the conditions producing them (slave). The impossibility of occupying both positions is not a defect in the system — it is the system’s operating principle. The feedback loop of self-regulation, by which the subject performs the savior role within the slave’s constraint, is itself what governance consists of.

item.title: Zen fascism
item.description: Zen fascism is the affective regime within californication in which detachment from crisis functions as ethical credential and distress registers as pathology. The term names what happens when the formatting of structural contradiction as personal affect becomes normative — when feeling rules graduate from descriptive social expectation to enforced hierarchy with real consequences. The serene subject is authorized to speak; the distressed subject is offered therapy. Those most affected by structural violence are least authorized to name it as structural, because naming it structurally is itself a violation of the regime’s feeling rules.

item.title: Coherent confusion
item.description: Coherent confusion is the system-level governance condition in which subjects are confused but functional — unable to resolve what is happening, yet still interpreting, engaging, and producing the sensemaking labor that sustains the system. The confusion is not a failure of governance. The confusion is governance. The interpretive labor of trying to make sense of contradictory signals — the takes, the threads, the analyses, the commentary — is itself what sustains system coherence.

item.title: Socially ontogenic media
item.description:
Socially ontogenic media is any media artifact that models a survivable identity posture within a contradictory, unstable, or collapsing system. Unlike media that entertains, explains, or persuades, socially ontogenic media provides its audience with a usable template for how to be a coherent actor inside a system that does not offer clear guidance on who to be. It distributes survivable selfhood.

A surgeon in a war zone cracks a joke while amputating a leg. The audience laughs — not because it is funny, but because M*A*S*H has spent seasons teaching them that laughter is how you survive a system that processes bodies without pause. The show does not explain how to cope with war. It does not argue a position on war. It models a way of being inside war’s contradictions: ironic, compassionate, rhythmically attuned to crisis that never resolves. The viewer does not learn a lesson. They internalize a posture.
